questions.question: Do mRNA vaccines change your DNA permanently?
questions.rationale: Addresses one of the most persistent and harmful COVID-19 vaccine misconceptions that continues to affect public health
questions.misinformation_narrative: Persistent false belief that mRNA vaccines alter human genetic material, despite extensive evidence they do not enter cell nuclei or integrate into DNA
questions.public_stakes: Vaccine hesitancy based on genetic modification fears reduces vaccination rates and increases disease transmission
nasem_sources_preview: Heritable Human Genome Editing (2021)
Human Genome Editing: Science, Ethics, and Governance (2017)
Adverse Effects of Vaccines: Evidence and Causality (2012)
The Childhood Immunization Schedule and Safety: Stakeholder Concerns, Scientific Evidence, and Future Studies (2013)
Immunization Safety Review: Influenza Vaccines and Neurological Complications (2004)

questions.question: Is naloxone (Narcan) safe for non-medical people to administer during overdoses?
questions.rationale: Addresses fears that prevent bystanders from using life-saving overdose reversal medication
questions.misinformation_narrative: Some people fear that using Narcan incorrectly could harm overdose victims or that only medical professionals should administer it
questions.public_stakes: Hesitation to use naloxone during overdoses leads to preventable deaths from opioid overdoses
nasem_sources_preview: Medications for Opioid Use Disorder Save Lives (2019)
Integrating Responses at the Intersection of Opioid Use Disorder and Infectious Disease Epidemics: Proceedings of a Workshop (2018)
Pain Management for People with Serious Illness in the Context of the Opioid Use Disorder Epidemic: Proceedings of a Workshop (2019)
Immunization Safety Review: Multiple Immunizations and Immune Dysfunction (2004)
Using Population Descriptors in Genetics and Genomics Research: A New Framework for an Evolving Field (2023)

questions.question: Can pharmacists provide medical care equivalent to doctors?
questions.rationale: Clarifies the scope and training of pharmacists amid confusion about their expanding healthcare roles
questions.misinformation_narrative: Some people believe pharmacists are just pill counters with minimal training, while others may overestimate their scope of practice compared to physicians
questions.public_stakes: Underutilizing pharmacist expertise leads to missed preventive care opportunities, while overestimating their role could delay necessary physician care
nasem_sources_preview: National Plan for Health Workforce Well-Being (2023)
Crossing the Quality Chasm: A New Health System for the 21st Century (2001)
Improving Diagnosis in Health Care (2016)
Medical Surge Capacity: Workshop Summary (2010)
Evolving Crisis Standards of Care and Ongoing Lessons from COVID-19: Proceedings of a Workshop Series (2023)

questions.question: Should elderly patients stop taking multiple medications to avoid harmful interactions?
questions.rationale: Addresses confusion about medication management in elderly populations where both under-treatment and over-treatment are risks
questions.misinformation_narrative: Many people believe that taking multiple medications is inherently dangerous for elderly patients, when evidence-based deprescribing requires careful medical evaluation
questions.public_stakes: Self-directed medication discontinuation can worsen chronic conditions, while inappropriate polypharmacy increases adverse events
nasem_sources_preview: Medications for Opioid Use Disorder Save Lives (2019)
Keeping Patients Safe: Transforming the Work Environment of Nurses (2004)
Crossing the Quality Chasm: A New Health System for the 21st Century (2001)
Taking Action Against Clinician Burnout: A Systems Approach to Professional Well-Being (2019)
Communities in Action: Pathways to Health Equity (2017)

questions.question: Is PrEP (pre-exposure prophylaxis) 100% effective at preventing HIV?
questions.rationale: Clarifies the high but not perfect effectiveness of PrEP to ensure appropriate use and expectations
questions.misinformation_narrative: Either overestimation of PrEP effectiveness leading to risky behavior, or underestimation leading to avoidance of this highly effective prevention tool
questions.public_stakes: Misconceptions about PrEP effectiveness can lead to HIV infections through either false confidence or missed prevention opportunities
nasem_sources_preview: Growing Up Tobacco Free: Preventing Nicotine Addiction in Children and Youths (1994)
Preventing Medication Errors (2007)
Bridge and Tunnel Strikes: A Guide for Prevention and Mitigation (2025)
Evidence-Based Practice for Public Health Emergency Preparedness and Response (2020)
Bridging the Evidence Gap in Obesity Prevention: A Framework to Inform Decision Making (2010)

questions.question: Are cancer screening tests always beneficial regardless of age or health status?
questions.rationale: Addresses misconceptions about universal benefits of cancer screening versus evidence-based guidelines
questions.misinformation_narrative: Belief that cancer screening is always beneficial leads to over-screening in low-risk individuals, while fear of screening leads to under-screening in appropriate candidates
questions.public_stakes: Over-screening causes unnecessary anxiety and procedures, while under-screening misses treatable cancers
nasem_sources_preview: Improving Diagnosis in Health Care (2016)
Ovarian Cancers: Evolving Paradigms in Research and Care (2016)
Childhood Cancer Survivorship: Improving Care and Quality of Life (2003)
The Drug Development Paradigm in Oncology: Proceedings of a Workshop (2018)
Health Literacy and Communication Strategies in Oncology: Proceedings of a Workshop (2020)

questions.question: Are children's medications safe when used off-label without FDA approval?
questions.rationale: Addresses widespread parental fear about off-label pediatric medication use, which affects treatment adherence and health outcomes
questions.misinformation_narrative: Many parents believe off-label medication use in children is dangerous or experimental, when in fact it's a necessary and often evidence-based practice for treating pediatric conditions
questions.public_stakes: Parents may refuse necessary treatments for their children, leading to untreated conditions and worse health outcomes
nasem_sources_preview: Challenges for the FDA: The Future of Drug Safety: Workshop Summary (2007)
Making Better Drugs for Children with Cancer (2005)
The Childhood Immunization Schedule and Safety: Stakeholder Concerns, Scientific Evidence, and Future Studies (2013)

Launching Lifelong Health by Improving Health Care for Children, Youth, and Families (2025)

questions.question: Are AI medical diagnostic systems already more accurate than human doctors?
questions.rationale: Addresses hype and misconceptions about current AI medical capabilities versus established evidence
questions.misinformation_narrative: Exaggerated claims that AI has already surpassed human physicians in diagnostic accuracy, leading to either over-reliance on AI or dismissal of its genuine benefits
questions.public_stakes: Premature replacement of physician judgment with AI could lead to diagnostic errors, while rejecting AI assistance could miss opportunities to improve care

Reproducibility and Replicability in Science (2019)

questions.question: Do GLP-1 medications like Ozempic only work for weight loss?
questions.rationale: Corrects oversimplified understanding of GLP-1 medications amid widespread media attention on weight loss effects
questions.misinformation_narrative: Public focus on GLP-1 medications as weight loss drugs obscures their primary cardiovascular and diabetes benefits, leading to misunderstanding of appropriate use
questions.public_stakes: Patients may seek these medications inappropriately for weight loss while missing their established medical benefits for diabetes and heart disease
nasem_sources_preview: The Challenge of Treating Obesity and Overweight: Proceedings of a Workshop (2018)

questions.question: Do discount prescription services like GoodRx actually save patients money?
questions.rationale: Clarifies how prescription discount services operate amid consumer confusion about pricing transparency
questions.misinformation_narrative: Confusion about how prescription discount services work and whether they provide genuine savings or hidden costs
questions.public_stakes: Patients may overpay for medications or miss better savings opportunities due to misunderstanding discount service models

questions.question: Will artificial intelligence systems automatically develop goals to survive and harm humans?
questions.rationale: Separates evidence-based AI safety concerns from science fiction scenarios in public discourse
questions.misinformation_narrative: Science fiction-driven fears that AI systems inevitably develop self-preservation instincts and become hostile to humans
questions.public_stakes: Unfounded fears may hinder beneficial AI development, while dismissing genuine AI safety concerns could lead to inadequate safeguards

questions.question: Are blood thinners safe for children with blood clots?
questions.rationale: Addresses parental concerns about pediatric anticoagulation therapy based on adult medication fears
questions.misinformation_narrative: Parents may fear giving blood thinners to children due to bleeding risks, not understanding that pediatric formulations and dosing have been specifically studied
questions.public_stakes: Delaying appropriate anticoagulation in children with blood clots can lead to life-threatening complications
